rows.notes: Top line labelled 'Brought up' and a large pounds figure. Pounds digits (3 0 4 4) are legible but the middle two digits are slightly ambiguous in the looped script; chosen reading 3044 because it aligns visually with the hundreds/thousands shape used elsewhere on the page.
Description text fairly clear ('Johnson' / 'smoke bills at Kidlington'). Numeric columns are well aligned; 4£ 7s 2d is unambiguous.

rows.description: Pates - Fees for Rectory at Riddington & Planting
rows.notes: Text partly faded/abbreviated (name looks like 'Pates' and place like 'Riddington'); numbers 10 19 3 align well but handwriting ambiguity on some letters causes moderate uncertainty in description.

rows.description: Parker - Books for Bishop of Montreal
rows.notes: Clear description and well-aligned numbers. '54 7 0' is crisp and shillings/pence in valid ranges.
Surname 'Rose' and 'late Kitchen Woman' readable; trailing note about 'injury / 3/4 wk' is less clear but the amount 13 0 0 is clearly written.
Phrase 'Rector's Improved Stipend' is clearly written and amount '100 0 0' is unambiguous and aligned with the pounds column.
'Do' (ditto) is clear but the following name is heavily stylized and hard to read — I read it as 'Mr Whelen' though alternative readings are possible. Numeric 10 0 0 is clear.
Ditto 'Do' then item looks like 'Postage & Parcels'; amount '3 15 2' is well aligned and clearly readable.

rows.description: Compensation to Fellows for Dry piece
rows.notes: Description mostly legible though 'Dry piece' could be an abbreviated phrase; numeric '22 6 7' is clear and within valid ranges.
Text 'Subs to Devonport Churches, 3d instalment' is readable; amount '25 0 0' is clear.

rows.description: Mr Walsh - Steward's Bill (including presentation & circulation)
rows.notes: Name and 'Steward's Bill' readable; there is a small marginal note in braces that I interpret as 'including presentation & circulation' but the marginal note is cramped and somewhat uncertain. Amount '70 0 0' clear.
Clear name and the numeric entry '20 14 0' is well aligned and unambiguous.
Word looks like 'Groce' (possibly 'Groceries') for Venison; amount '5 17 0' is clear in columns.
Read as 'Carriage to Pew and back' — the second word might be 'Pew'; amount '2 6 6' is clearly written and aligned.

rows.description: Capel - bile for stating born at Lampsfield
rows.notes: Handwriting for the name and explanation is very unclear (I read 'Capel - bile for stating born at Lampsfield' as the closest match). Numeric '12 2 6' is clear, but the textual reading is low confidence.
Surname 'Ladd' and '100 Thanksgiving prayers' readable; amount is small (0£ 6s 6d) and clearly in the shilling/pence columns.

rows.description: Clunan Devonport, Rec't for circulars respecting admission
rows.notes: Name looks like 'Clunan' or 'Climan' of Devonport; phrase 'Rec't for circulars respecting admission' is inferred from abbreviation and context. Amount '0 6 0' is visible but the name is moderately ambiguous.
Clear phrase 'Carrying small coal' and amount '1 0 0' is neatly written and unambiguous.

rows.description: Mr Shephard - Parsonage Stipend to Mitchelland
rows.notes: Name and following note are heavily abbreviated/stylized. I read the name as 'Mr Shephard' and the recipient/place as 'Mitchelland' but alternate readings are possible. Numeric '12 10 0' is clear.
This is a clear sectional header introducing the list of tradesmen; there are no monetary amounts on the header line.

Short line and marginal reference 'see fol. 69' visible. The firm name is faint ('Galgas' or 'Sulgas' possible). Amount digits are small and slightly smudged; I interpret them as '5 11 4' but this reading is low confidence.
Firm name 'Gille & Ward' is clear; amount '50 19 4' is neatly written and unambiguous (shillings 19 valid).
Name 'Lynn' and 'Carpenter &c' legible; figures '22 3 1' clear and pass column validation.
Clear 'Dudley - Plumber &c'; numeric '17 8 6' is unambiguous.
Name 'Knowles - Mason' readable; amount '83 18 3' clearly written and within valid ranges.
Name 'Nimney - Plasterer &c' readable. Pence read as '8 1/2' (there is a halfpenny mark / loop after the '8'); fractional 0.5 interpreted per page convention. Columns align well.
Final total line 'Carried over' with pounds '3621', shillings '10', pence '3 1/2' clearly written; halfpenny symbol visible. Column alignment used to confirm vertical rulings.
